---
title: "RegionSet S4 Class"
subtitle: "gtars genomic interval analysis with Rust-backed S4 objects"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

# Overview

gtars provides a `RegionSet` S4 class that wraps Rust-backed genomic interval
data. It supports:

- Construction from BED files, GRanges, or data.frames
- Strand support: preserved from GRanges and data.frames, round-trips correctly
- Statistics: `widths()`, `neighborDistances()`, `nearestNeighbors()`
- Interval operations: `reduce()`, `union()`, `setdiff()`, `trim()`, etc.
- Drop-in compatibility with GenomicDistributions plotting functions

```{r load}
library(gtars)
```

# Creating a RegionSet

## From a BED file

```{r from-file}
data_dir <- normalizePath(file.path("..", "..", "tests", "data", "regionset"))
rs <- RegionSet(file.path(data_dir, "dummy.bed"))
rs
```

## From a data.frame

Data.frames use 0-based half-open (BED) coordinates.

```{r from-df}
df <- data.frame(
  chr   = c("chr1", "chr1", "chr2"),
  start = c(100L, 500L, 0L),
  end   = c(200L, 600L, 300L)
)
rs_df <- RegionSet(df)
rs_df
```

## From GRanges

GRanges coordinates (1-based closed) are automatically converted.

```{r from-granges, eval=requireNamespace("GenomicRanges", quietly=TRUE)}
library(GenomicRanges)
gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1", "chr2"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(101, 501, 1), end = c(200, 600, 300))
)
rs_gr <- RegionSet(gr)
rs_gr

# Round-trip back to GRanges
as_granges(rs_gr)
```

# Strand support

RegionSet carries strand information as `"+"`, `"-"`, or `"*"` (unstranded).

## From a stranded data.frame

```{r strand-df}
df_stranded <- data.frame(
  chr    = c("chr1", "chr1", "chr2"),
  start  = c(100L, 500L, 0L),
  end    = c(200L, 600L, 300L),
  strand = c("+", "-", "+")
)
rs_stranded <- RegionSet(df_stranded)
rs_stranded
```

## From stranded GRanges

Strand is automatically extracted from GRanges objects.

```{r strand-granges, eval=requireNamespace("GenomicRanges", quietly=TRUE)}
gr_stranded <- GRanges(
  seqnames = c("chr1", "chr1", "chr2"),
  ranges = IRanges(start = c(101, 501, 1), end = c(200, 600, 300)),
  strand = c("+", "-", "+")
)
rs_from_gr <- RegionSet(gr_stranded)
rs_from_gr

# Round-trip preserves strand
as_granges(rs_from_gr)
```

## Strand in as.data.frame

The strand column is always included in the data.frame output.

```{r strand-as-df}
as.data.frame(rs_stranded)
```

## Strand through operations

Positional operations (`promoters`, `concat`, `pintersect`) preserve strand.
Merge operations (`reduce`, `union`, `setdiff`, `trim`) reset strand to `"*"`
because they create new regions.

```{r strand-ops}
# promoters preserves strand
as.data.frame(promoters(rs_stranded, upstream = 50L, downstream = 10L))

# concat preserves strand from both inputs
rs_plus <- RegionSet(data.frame(
  chr = "chr3", start = 0L, end = 50L, strand = "+"
))
as.data.frame(concat(rs_stranded, rs_plus))

# reduce loses strand (regions are merged)
as.data.frame(reduce(rs_stranded))
```

## BED files

BED files loaded via file path default to `"*"` (unstranded). Strand is shown
in the display only when at least one region has `"+"` or `"-"`.

```{r strand-file}
rs_file <- RegionSet(file.path(data_dir, "dummy.bed"))
rs_file
```

# Basic methods

```{r basic}
length(rs_df)
as.data.frame(rs_df)
```

# Statistics

## Region widths

```{r widths}
widths(rs)

# GenomicDistributions drop-in:
calcWidth(rs)
```

## Neighbor distances

```{r neighbors}
neighborDistances(rs)
nearestNeighbors(rs)
```

## Chromosome statistics

```{r chrom-stats}
chromosomeStatistics(rs_df)
```

# Interval operations

## reduce — merge overlapping regions

```{r reduce}
rs
reduce(rs)
as.data.frame(reduce(rs))
```

## union, setdiff

```{r set-ops}
a <- RegionSet(data.frame(
  chr = "chr1", start = c(0L, 20L), end = c(10L, 30L)
))
b <- RegionSet(data.frame(
  chr = "chr1", start = 5L, end = 25L
))

as.data.frame(union(a, b))
as.data.frame(setdiff(a, b))
```

## concat — combine without merging

```{r concat}
ab <- concat(a, b)
length(ab)
as.data.frame(ab)
```

## trim — clip to chromosome boundaries

```{r trim}
chrom_sizes <- c(chr1 = 8L)
as.data.frame(rs)
as.data.frame(trim(rs, chromSizes = chrom_sizes))
```

## promoters

```{r promoters}
as.data.frame(promoters(a, upstream = 50L, downstream = 10L))
```

# Jaccard similarity

```{r jaccard}
jaccard(a, b)
jaccard(a, a)  # self-similarity

# Disjoint sets
c <- RegionSet(data.frame(chr = "chr2", start = 100L, end = 200L))
jaccard(a, c)
```

# Consensus regions

Find regions present in k-of-n input sets.

```{r consensus}
consensus(list(a, b))
consensus(list(a, b, c))
```

# Mixed input types

All methods accept GRanges, file paths, data.frames, or RegionSet objects.
At least one argument must be a RegionSet for binary BiocGenerics methods
(`union`, `setdiff`).

```{r mixed}
# widths on a data.frame directly
widths(data.frame(chr = "chr1", start = c(0L, 100L), end = c(50L, 200L)))

# jaccard with mixed types
jaccard(
  data.frame(chr = "chr1", start = 0L, end = 100L),
  data.frame(chr = "chr1", start = 50L, end = 150L)
)

# concat file path + data.frame
rs_file <- RegionSet(file.path(data_dir, "dummy.bed"))
rs_mixed <- concat(rs_file, data.frame(chr = "chr2", start = 0L, end = 100L))
as.data.frame(rs_mixed)
```

# Drop-in for GenomicDistributions

The `calc*` functions accept GRanges directly, so existing GenomicDistributions
scripts work by swapping `library(GenomicDistributions)` for `library(gtars)`.

```{r drop-in, eval=requireNamespace("GenomicRanges", quietly=TRUE)}
# Existing workflow — unchanged:
calcWidth(gr)
calcNeighborDist(gr)
calcNearestNeighbors(gr)
```

Multi-object functions like `calcPartitions`, `calcGCContent`, etc. also
accept both GRanges and RegionSet.
